_meta.disclaimer: These rulings reflect commonly-accepted positions within each school. Individual scholars may differ. Always verify with a qualified scholar from your madhab before relying on these for ritual decisions.

rulings.topic: Wudu (ritual purity) for Tawaf
rulings.hanafi: Wajib (necessary). If wudu breaks during Tawaf, it should be resumed after renewing wudu. Performing Tawaf without wudu is sinful but the Tawaf is valid; a dam (sacrifice) becomes obligatory.
rulings.shafi: Fard (essential pillar). Tawaf is invalid without wudu and must be repeated.
rulings.maliki: Fard (essential pillar). Tawaf is invalid without wudu and must be repeated.
rulings.hanbali: Fard (essential pillar). Tawaf is invalid without wudu and must be repeated.

rulings.topic: Deadline for Tawaf al-Ziyarah (Tawaf al-Ifadah)
rulings.hanafi: Must be performed within the days of Nahr (10th, 11th, or 12th of Dhul Hijjah, before sunset of the 12th). Delaying past this incurs a dam.
rulings.shafi: Begins after midnight of the 10th. No final deadline — remains valid even if delayed for years, though delaying without excuse is disliked.
rulings.maliki: Must be performed within the days of Tashreeq (up to sunset of the 13th). Delaying beyond incurs a dam.
rulings.hanbali: No fixed deadline — remains valid even if delayed, though doing it within the days of Hajj is preferred.

rulings.topic: Time for stoning (Rami) on the 11th and 12th of Dhul Hijjah
rulings.hanafi: Must be after Zawal (true noon). Stoning before Zawal on these days is not valid and must be repeated.
rulings.shafi: Must be after Zawal (true noon). Stoning before Zawal on these days is not valid.
rulings.maliki: Must be after Zawal (true noon). Stoning before Zawal on these days is not valid.
rulings.hanbali: Preferably after Zawal, but some Hanbali scholars permit stoning before Zawal on the 12th and 13th if leaving Mina early.

rulings.topic: Combining Dhuhr and Asr at Arafah
rulings.hanafi: Combining is only valid when prayed behind the Imam at Masjid Namirah in congregation. Otherwise, each prayer is offered at its own time.
rulings.shafi: Combining is sunnah at Arafah for all pilgrims, whether in congregation or alone. Dhuhr and Asr are joined at Dhuhr time (jam' taqdeem).
rulings.maliki: Combining is sunnah at Arafah for all pilgrims, joined at Dhuhr time.
rulings.hanbali: Combining is sunnah at Arafah for all pilgrims, joined at Dhuhr time.

rulings.topic: Combining Maghrib and Isha at Muzdalifah
rulings.hanafi: Wajib to delay Maghrib and pray it together with Isha at Isha's time at Muzdalifah. Praying Maghrib before reaching Muzdalifah requires repetition.
rulings.shafi: Sunnah to combine at Isha's time (jam' ta'kheer). Praying separately is permitted but contrary to sunnah.
rulings.maliki: Sunnah to combine at Isha's time at Muzdalifah.
rulings.hanbali: Sunnah to combine at Isha's time at Muzdalifah.

rulings.topic: Shortening prayers (Qasr) for travelers
rulings.hanafi: Wajib (obligatory) for travelers — four-rakah prayers must be shortened to two. A traveler who intends to stay 15 days or more in one place becomes a resident.
rulings.shafi: Permissible (rukhsah). Either shortening or praying in full is valid. Threshold for becoming resident is 4 days (excluding day of arrival/departure).
rulings.maliki: Strongly recommended (sunnah mu'akkadah). Threshold for becoming resident is 4 days.
rulings.hanbali: Recommended (sunnah). Threshold for becoming resident is more than 4 days.

rulings.topic: Tawaf al-Wada (Farewell Tawaf)
rulings.hanafi: Wajib for non-residents of Makkah. Skipping it incurs a dam. Menstruating women are exempt.
rulings.shafi: Wajib for all pilgrims (including residents in some opinions). Skipping it incurs a dam. Menstruating women are exempt.
rulings.maliki: Sunnah, not wajib. No dam if skipped.
rulings.hanbali: Wajib for all non-residents. Skipping incurs a dam. Menstruating women are exempt.

rulings.topic: Length of hair to cut for Taqsir (shortening)
rulings.hanafi: At least one fingertip-length (~2.5cm) cut from at least one quarter of the head.
rulings.shafi: Cutting any three hairs is sufficient as a minimum, though more is preferred.
rulings.maliki: Must shorten across the entire head, not just a portion.
rulings.hanbali: Must shorten across the entire head, not just a portion.

rulings.topic: Halq (full shaving) vs Taqsir (shortening) for men
rulings.hanafi: Halq is preferred (afdal). Taqsir is permitted but yields lesser reward.
rulings.shafi: Halq is preferred. Taqsir is permitted.
rulings.maliki: Halq is preferred. Taqsir is permitted.
rulings.hanbali: Halq is preferred. Taqsir is permitted.

rulings.topic: Order of rituals on 10th Dhul Hijjah
rulings.hanafi: The order is wajib: Rami of Jamarat al-Aqabah → Sacrifice (if applicable) → Halq/Taqsir → Tawaf al-Ziyarah. Doing them out of order incurs a dam.
rulings.shafi: The order is sunnah, not wajib. Performing out of order is permitted with no penalty.
rulings.maliki: The order is sunnah. Performing out of order is permitted.
rulings.hanbali: The order is sunnah. No penalty for variation.

rulings.topic: Leaving Mina early (Nafr Awwal) on the 12th
rulings.hanafi: Must depart before sunset of the 12th. If sunset arrives while still in Mina, staying for the 13th and stoning becomes obligatory.
rulings.shafi: Must depart before sunset of the 12th. If caught by sunset, staying for the 13th becomes obligatory.
rulings.maliki: Must depart before sunset of the 12th. If caught by sunset, staying for the 13th becomes obligatory.
rulings.hanbali: Must depart before sunset of the 12th. If caught by sunset, staying for the 13th becomes obligatory.

rulings.topic: Takbir at-Tashreeq after fard prayers
rulings.hanafi: Wajib once after every fard prayer from Fajr of the 9th to Asr of the 13th of Dhul Hijjah (23 prayers total). Recited audibly by men, silently by women.
rulings.shafi: Sunnah after every prayer from Maghrib of the 9th to Asr of the 13th. Some opinions extend it.
rulings.maliki: Sunnah after fard prayers from Dhuhr of the 10th to Fajr of the 13th.
rulings.hanbali: Sunnah after fard prayers from Fajr of the 9th to Asr of the 13th.

rulings.topic: Covering the face for women in Ihram
rulings.hanafi: Cloth must not touch the face. Covering with a face-veil supported by a cap or frame (so it doesn't touch the skin) is permitted and recommended in front of non-mahram men.
rulings.shafi: The face must remain uncovered during Ihram. Covering with anything that touches the face invalidates the state until removed.
rulings.maliki: Face must be uncovered. May lower a cloth in front of non-mahram men if it doesn't directly touch the face.
rulings.hanbali: Face must be uncovered. May lower a cloth in front of non-mahram men if it doesn't directly touch the face.

rulings.topic: Starting Sa'i at Safa
rulings.hanafi: Must start at Safa. Starting at Marwah invalidates the first lap, which must be repeated.
rulings.shafi: Must start at Safa. Starting at Marwah invalidates that lap.
rulings.maliki: Must start at Safa. Starting at Marwah invalidates that lap.
rulings.hanbali: Must start at Safa. Starting at Marwah invalidates that lap.

rulings.topic: Time required at Arafah (Wuquf)
rulings.hanafi: Any portion of time between Zawal of the 9th and Fajr of the 10th. Even a moment counts. Combining day and night is sunnah.
rulings.shafi: Any portion between Zawal of the 9th and Fajr of the 10th. Combining day and night is sunnah.
rulings.maliki: Must include some portion of the night between the 9th and 10th. Day-only Wuquf without night is invalid and incurs a dam.
rulings.hanbali: Any portion between Fajr of the 9th and Fajr of the 10th. Combining day and night is preferred.

rulings.topic: Staying overnight at Muzdalifah
rulings.hanafi: Wajib to stop at Muzdalifah, even briefly, between Fajr time and sunrise of the 10th. Skipping incurs a dam.
rulings.shafi: Wajib to stop at Muzdalifah for any portion of the second half of the night.
rulings.maliki: Wajib to stop and pray Maghrib and Isha there. Brief stop after the prayers is sufficient.
rulings.hanbali: Wajib to stop until at least midnight or until Fajr.

rulings.topic: Sacrifice (Hady) for Tamattu' and Qiran pilgrims
rulings.hanafi: Wajib. If unable, must fast 3 days during Hajj and 7 after returning home (10 total).
rulings.shafi: Wajib. Same fasting alternative if unable.
rulings.maliki: Wajib. Same fasting alternative if unable.
rulings.hanbali: Wajib. Same fasting alternative if unable.

rulings.topic: Ramal (brisk walking) and Idtiba (uncovering shoulder) in Tawaf
rulings.hanafi: Sunnah for men in the first three of seven circuits of Tawaf al-Qudum and Tawaf following Sa'i. Idtiba is sunnah throughout that Tawaf.
rulings.shafi: Sunnah for men in the same circumstances. Idtiba is sunnah throughout.
rulings.maliki: Sunnah for men. Practiced similarly.
rulings.hanbali: Sunnah for men. Practiced similarly.

rulings.topic: Menstruating women and Tawaf
rulings.hanafi: Forbidden to perform Tawaf while menstruating. Must wait until pure. Tawaf al-Wada is waived. If Tawaf al-Ziyarah is delayed past the deadline due to menses, dam becomes obligatory but does not invalidate Hajj.
rulings.shafi: Forbidden. Must wait. Tawaf al-Wada is waived. No deadline pressure for Tawaf al-Ziyarah.
rulings.maliki: Forbidden. Must wait. Tawaf al-Wada is waived (and not wajib in this school anyway).
rulings.hanbali: Forbidden. Must wait. Tawaf al-Wada is waived.

rulings.topic: Place of intention (Niyyah) for Ihram
rulings.hanafi: Must enter the state of Ihram at or before the Miqat. Crossing the Miqat without Ihram requires returning to it or paying a dam.
rulings.shafi: Same — Ihram at or before the Miqat. Crossing without Ihram requires return or dam.
rulings.maliki: Same — Ihram at or before the Miqat. Crossing without Ihram requires return or dam.
rulings.hanbali: Same — Ihram at or before the Miqat. Crossing without Ihram requires return or dam.
